steps.title: Identify the passage’s central focus
steps.content: The passage opens with Mariana startled awake by a news report, unsure when she fell asleep. It then draws a deliberate contrast: on most nights sleep came “quickly”. The next sentence is “Violently.” But last night she waded into it “slowly, almost willingly” — like moonlight inviting you into a receding tide. The passage is built around Mariana’s awakening and that contrast in how she fell asleep.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Check each choice against the passage’s actual content:
  • Choice A — How Mariana has awakened and how she fell asleep earlier: Matches both elements — her startled awakening by the news report and the extended contrast between her usual violent sleep and last night’s slow, willing drift. ✓
  • Choice B — Why Mariana only watches television news before going to bed: The television news appears only as the trigger for her awakening; the passage never explains why she watches it or suggests it is a habit. ✗
  • Choice C — How Mariana is enjoying the change in the weather: The clouds are a brief atmospheric detail in the opening line; Mariana’s enjoyment of weather is never the subject. ✗
  • Choice D — What Mariana expects to do the following day: The passage contains no mention of Mariana’s plans or expectations for the next day. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
